target.title: fix: preserve plugin LLM command auth
target.body:
## What

Preserves host-bound agent auth profile and model resolution for plugin LLM command/runtime calls and context-engine compactions, including text, Telegram, and Discord native command paths.

## Why

Plugin commands and compactions can run inside an existing session whose selected model/auth profile differs from the default. Without carrying the trusted session binding into the LLM runtime, these paths can fall back to the wrong auth profile or fail to bind an active agent.

## Changes

- Bind context-engine compaction auth
- Add plugin command LLM runtime
- Propagate Telegram command auth
- Propagate Discord command auth
- Keep auth selection host-owned
- Preserve static model resolution
- Cover legacy main sessions
- Add changelog entry

## Testing

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/runtime/runtime-llm.runtime.test.ts src/plugins/commands.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-plugin.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.session-meta.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/native-command.plugin-dispatch.test.ts`
- `pnpm build`
- `AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main`
- Live rebuilt gateway proof below

Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: OpenClaw plugin runtime LLM completions preserve host-resolved Codex auth when a plugin invokes `runtime.llm.complete` through a command path.
Real environment tested: Live OpenClaw gateway rebuilt and restarted from the patched branch, with a real plugin command exercising the plugin SDK runtime LLM surface from Telegram.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran `/lossless doctor apply`, then inspected `/tmp/openclaw/openclaw-2026-05-25.log` for the post-restart run around `2026-05-25T08:56-08:57`.
Evidence after fix: The plugin LLM request was routed through OpenClaw's transport-aware Codex path: `provider=openai-codex api=openclaw-openai-responses-transport model=gpt-5.5`, `url=https://chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex/responses`, redacted resolved auth present as `apiKey=***`, HTTP `status=200`, first SSE event `response.created`, and final `stream_done ... response.completed ... stopReason=stop`.
Observed result after fix: OpenClaw logged a successful `plugin llm completion` for `caller.kind=plugin`, with `purpose=lossless-claw compaction summarization`, `totalTokens=9608`, and no post-restart `runtime.llm.complete error`, `provider_error`, `ALL PROVIDERS EXHAUSTED`, or `Provider openai-codex: "apiKey" or "oauth" is required` entries.
What was not tested: This proof covers OpenClaw's plugin command/runtime LLM auth and transport path with a live Codex-backed request. It does not claim to tune plugin-level summarizer timeout behavior; the first plugin summarizer attempt timed out at 60s before a fallback request completed successfully.

target.title: fix(tavily): pass api_key in request body instead of Authorization header
target.body:
## Problem

When Tavily is configured as the web search provider (`tools.web.search.provider: "tavily"`) with a valid API key, all `web_search` calls return **0 results** with no error message. This affects all agents including family bots, security research, and any workflow using web search.

### Root Cause

OpenClaw sends the Tavily API key via `Authorization: Bearer <key>` header. Tavily's API **silently ignores** this header — it expects `api_key` in the request body. Since no error is returned, the search "succeeds" with `count: 0`.

The `postTrustedWebToolsJson` helper always puts the key in an `Authorization` header, which works for Brave/Perplexity but not Tavily.

### Fix

In `postTavilyJson`, spread `api_key` into the request body before forwarding:

```diff
- body: params.body,
+ body: { ...params.body, api_key: params.apiKey },
```

This ensures the API key reaches Tavily in the format it expects.

### Verification

- **Before:** `web_search("Metformin side effects")` → `count: 0` (no error)
- **After:** `web_search("Metformin side effects")` → `count: 3` (NHS, GoodRx, etc.) in 64ms

Direct API test confirms Tavily works with `api_key` in body but returns empty when only Bearer auth is used.

target.title: Redesign Overview as an operator-first OpenClaw dashboard
target.body:
## Summary
- Rework the connected Overview page into an operator-first dashboard instead of a setup/status landing page.
- Keep first-run Gateway Access and Snapshot cards intact for disconnected installs, then move them below daily operating signals once the Control UI is connected.
- Use existing gateway, session, cron, auth, provider/quota, connector, skill, and log data. This PR intentionally avoids adding new Gateway RPCs.

## Major functional improvements
- Makes the Overview useful at a glance: the first screen now answers what needs attention, what just ran, whether model/provider usage looks healthy, and whether cron/connectors/skills/logs look normal.
- Reduces dashboard scrolling: Attention and Recent Sessions stop growing without bound, and the usage/quota surface now uses a compact metric layout instead of a tall card.
- Respects OpenClaw's multi-model posture: the dashboard presents provider/quota health from the data available in the install, without implying OpenClaw is tied to one LLM provider.
- Shows clearer provider-limit detail when providers expose different kinds of limits: time buckets, model-tier buckets, token budgets, credit balances, and monthly quotas use provider-reported labels instead of a generic "quota" tile.
- Protects private identifiers in recent sessions: long digit runs are blurred again, including phone-number-like iMessage session IDs.
- Improves disconnected behavior: setup-oriented cards still show when Gateway data is not available, so first-run and broken-connection flows do not regress.
- Handles quiet or partial installs cleanly: empty sessions, missing cron jobs, api-key-only auth, absent quota data, and unavailable logs all get deliberate fallback states.

## Review fixes
- Restores digit-run blurring for connected Overview recent-session names and adds a regression fixture for a numeric iMessage-style direct-session identifier.
- Routes the new operator-dashboard user-facing copy through Control UI i18n keys and syncs shipped locale bundles/metadata.
- Replaces the Codex-specific usage card title with provider/model usage wording while still showing concrete provider names and provider-reported quota labels when providers supply usage data.
- Uses quota data from any provider that reports it, including API-key providers, while keeping the separate auth-health card focused on OAuth/token providers that can expire. Self-describing labels like OpenRouter `Credits` are no longer forced into `Credits quota`.
- Applies the same provider-native quota formatting to the chat header pill, so OpenRouter-style `Credits` renders with native remaining labels such as `$12.34 left` instead of `Credits quota` plus a normalized percentage.
- Adds provider-native quota display labels to the shared usage model, so balance providers can show dollar/credit amounts while percentage fields still support sorting and pressure ranking.
- Adds OpenRouter usage fetching for `/api/v1/credits` and `/api/v1/key`, displaying account credits/key limits as dollar balances when OpenRouter returns them.
- Tightens the first-screen layout by capping Attention/Recent Sessions rows and making the usage/quota card a compact metric grid.

## Screenshots
Redacted connected Overview screenshot from the current branch UI. This local Gateway capture currently returned no provider quota limits, so it shows the deliberate no-quota fallback state:

![Redacted connected OpenClaw Overview dashboard screenshot](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/homer-byte/openclaw/pr-assets/86460/.github/pr-assets/86460/openclaw-overview-pr-proof-redacted.png)

Redacted provider-quota fixture showing an OpenRouter-style dollar credits balance alongside a Codex 5h reset and a Z.ai token window. This fixture illustrates provider-shaped quota rendering without claiming the local install had live OpenRouter quota data at capture time:

![Redacted OpenRouter-style provider quota fixture screenshot](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/homer-byte/openclaw/pr-assets/86460/.github/pr-assets/86460/openclaw-overview-openrouter-quota-redacted.png)

## Validation
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/infra/provider-usage.format.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.shared.test.ts extensions/openrouter/index.test.ts extensions/openrouter/usage.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/overview.render.test.ts`
- `pnpm ui:i18n:sync`
- `pnpm ui:i18n:check`
- `pnpm format:docs:check`
- `pnpm ui:build`
- `pnpm lint:ui:no-raw-window-open`
- `pnpm build`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Connected Control UI Overview should show the compact operator dashboard while preserving privacy for recent-session identifiers, avoiding unbounded first-screen card height, and presenting model/provider usage without making OpenClaw look Codex-only or forcing credit/balance providers into percentage-only labels.
- **Real environment tested:** Local working OpenClaw Gateway at `http://127.0.0.1:18789/`, with the current branch Control UI bundle served locally for capture. The running installed Gateway was older than the branch, so provider usage was also verified directly through the current branch usage loader.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Built the repo, ran focused Overview/chat render tests plus i18n/build/diff checks, opened Overview with Playwright against the local Gateway, redacted private text in the DOM, and captured screenshots. A second redacted fixture patches the visible provider-usage panel to illustrate OpenRouter dollar-credit display alongside a Codex reset window and a Z.ai token window.
- **Evidence after fix:** The current branch provider-usage loader returned real Codex windows:

```json
{
  "provider": "openai-codex",
  "displayName": "Codex",
  "windows": [
    { "label": "5h", "usedPercent": 10 },
    { "label": "Week", "usedPercent": 15 }
  ]
}
```

The refreshed connected Overview screenshot rendered the no-quota fallback when the local Gateway returned no provider limits:

```text
QUOTA
unavailable
Model auth loaded, but no provider quota limits were returned; showing local session usage.
```

The OpenRouter/Codex render fixture now shows dollar remaining labels and a Codex reset row without repeating that reset in a footer:

```text
CREDITS
12%
OpenRouter · Credits    $12.34 left
Codex · 5h              reset 2h 9m   15% left
Z.ai · Tokens (6h)      reset 2h 59m  60% left
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The connected Overview rendered the operator dashboard and the provider-quota fallback matched the local Gateway data. The OpenRouter/Codex fixture demonstrates provider-native dollar balances and Codex reset rows while retaining normalized percentages for ranking and for providers whose APIs report percentage/count windows. The redundant prose footer is suppressed when provider quota rows already carry the provider/limit/reset details.
- **What was not tested:** No live OpenRouter account balance was queried on the local install; the OpenRouter screenshot remains a labeled fixture. Broader cross-browser visual QA remains ordinary maintainer review scope.

## Implementation notes
- This PR intentionally avoids new Gateway RPCs. Message queue state, update posture, and host-health exceptions are represented only when existing dashboard data can support them.
- Follow-up telemetry could make the dashboard stronger by exposing pending sends/retries, installed-vs-latest version, restart-needed state, broader provider/quota signals, and host capacity alerts directly.

target.title: refactor: use Rastermill for image processing
target.body:
## Summary

- Move OpenClaw image processing to the published npm package `rastermill@0.1.0`.
- Delete the in-repo image processor implementation from `src/media/image-ops.ts`; OpenClaw now uses a small Rastermill adapter while keeping trusted native command resolution.
- Preserve input/output pixel limits, `OPENCLAW_IMAGE_BACKEND`, Windows/native backend aliases, best-effort EXIF normalization, and central availability-error classification.

## Verification

- Rastermill npm: `npm view rastermill@0.1.0 name version dist.tarball dist.integrity dist.unpackedSize dependencies --json`
- Rastermill package before publish: `pnpm check && pnpm test && pnpm build && npm pack --dry-run`
- Rastermill publish: `npm publish --access public` published `rastermill@0.1.0`
- Rastermill contract follow-up: `pnpm test`; `pnpm check && pnpm build` after adding the backend fallback-stop test on `openclaw/rastermill@4045362`
- OpenClaw focused tests: `pnpm test src/media/image-ops.security.test.ts src/media/image-ops.rastermill-adapter.test.ts src/media/image-ops.input-guard.test.ts src/media/image-ops.helpers.test.ts src/media/web-media.test.ts src/agents/tool-images.test.ts src/gateway/managed-image-attachments.test.ts test/package-manager-config.test.ts test/scripts/check-dependency-pins.test.ts test/scripts/generate-npm-shrinkwrap.test.ts`
- OpenClaw TUI PTY: `timeout 120s node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.tui-pty.config.ts`
- OpenClaw build: `env -u OPENCLAW_TESTBOX pnpm build`
- Auto review: `autoreview --mode local` -> clean, no accepted/actionable findings

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: OpenClaw no longer owns its image-processing Photon implementation directly; core media image operations delegate to the public npm package `rastermill@0.1.0` while preserving safety and backend behavior.
Real environment tested: macOS local checkout, Node/pnpm repo defaults, real `rastermill@0.1.0` dependency fetched from npm.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm install --lockfile-only`; `node scripts/generate-npm-shrinkwrap.mjs`; focused `pnpm test ...`; `timeout 120s node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.tui-pty.config.ts`; `env -u OPENCLAW_TESTBOX pnpm build`; `autoreview --mode local`; npm registry verification for `rastermill@0.1.0`; Rastermill package `pnpm test`, `pnpm check`, and `pnpm build`.
Evidence after fix: npm reports `rastermill@0.1.0` at `https://registry.npmjs.org/rastermill/-/rastermill-0.1.0.tgz` with integrity `sha512-ovbl5jVRMc0LnzktQMU13Nz5c9iPbHi9PoT4hG8qiyOGVM6a20I6N2TtaFJv40B38LLF29luN/E8NmNIXk7lCg==`; focused OpenClaw tests passed 5 Vitest shards; TUI PTY passed 11 tests; build completed; Rastermill tests/check/build passed; autoreview was clean.
Observed result after fix: image metadata/resizing/PNG optimization/agent attachment paths remain green, native fallback commands still route through OpenClaw trusted resolution, shrinkwraps resolve the public npm package, and the TUI fixture can resolve Rastermill through tsx/Node loader probing. Rastermill now has a package-level contract test proving real Photon processing errors do not fall through to native fallback tools.
What was not tested: Windows `windows-native` PowerShell execution was preserved in Rastermill and typechecked, but not live-run on a Windows host in this local pass. `pnpm check:changed` currently reaches unrelated latest-main type errors in `src/agents/agent-command.live-model-switch.test.ts` and `src/tui/embedded-backend.test.ts`; those files are not changed by this PR.

target.title: Doctor: expose UI freshness health findings
target.body:
## Summary

- Exposes Control UI protocol freshness as a structured doctor health check for lint and dry-run repair effects.
- Keeps real build/rebuild mutation on the existing `maybeRepairUiProtocolFreshness()` path used by `openclaw doctor --fix`.
- Keeps existing doctor repairs in their positional slots: the pre-health UI freshness step still calls the legacy repair path, and the broad structured runner now excludes positional-owned health checks.
- Hardens the stale detector so a successful empty git history does not create false stale findings, while unavailable git history still reports the mtime mismatch without commit details.
- Skips build/rebuild prompts when the checkout does not contain `ui/` sources.

This PR does not change the public health/plugin SDK contract, does not invent file diffs for generated UI assets, and does not move existing doctor repair order into the broad structured runner.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
`openclaw doctor --lint` can report missing/stale Control UI assets, and dry-run repair output can preview the build/rebuild process effect without mutating generated UI files or changing the existing `openclaw doctor --fix` repair ownership.

Real environment tested:
WSL Ubuntu 24.04 durable OpenClaw worktree at `/root/src/openclaw-ui-freshness-stack`, head `f2a6b4af48`, source harness using the real doctor health registry and temporary ignored `dist/control-ui/index.html` states. Paths below are redacted.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. `git diff --check`
2. `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/commands/doctor-ui.ts src/commands/doctor-ui.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-conversion-plan.ts`
3. `node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/commands/doctor-ui.ts src/commands/doctor-ui.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-conversion-plan.ts`
4. `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-ui.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts --reporter=dot`
5. `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --noEmit --incremental false --pretty false`
6. `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --noEmit --incremental false --pretty false`
7. `node --import tsx /tmp/84290-real-proof.mjs`
8. `bash /mnt/c/src/claws-hapi/.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`

Evidence after fix:
Focused validation passed: oxfmt reported all matched files use the correct format; oxlint reported 0 warnings and 0 errors; Vitest reported 5 files and 47 tests passed; core and core-test tsgo exited 0; autoreview reported `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`.

Redacted behavior proof output:

```text

{"ok":false,"checksRun":1,"checksSkipped":55,"findings":[{"checkId":"core/doctor/ui-protocol-freshness","severity":"warning","message":"- Control UI assets are missing.\n- Run: pnpm ui:build","path":"/redacted/openclaw/dist/control-ui/index.html","fixHint":"Run `openclaw doctor --fix` to build Control UI assets."}]}
--- missing assets dry-run repair result ---
{
  "checksRun": 1,
  "checksRepaired": 1,
  "findings": [
    {
      "checkId": "core/doctor/ui-protocol-freshness",
      "severity": "warning",
      "message": "- Control UI assets are missing.\n- Run: pnpm ui:build",
      "path": "/redacted/openclaw/dist/control-ui/index.html",
      "fixHint": "Run `openclaw doctor --fix` to build Control UI assets."
    }
  ],
  "effects": [
    {
      "kind": "process",
      "action": "would-build-control-ui",
      "target": "/redacted/openclaw",
      "dryRunSafe": false
    }
  ],
  "changes": [],
  "warnings": []
}
--- stale assets lint JSON ---
exitCode=1
{"ok":false,"checksRun":1,"checksSkipped":18,"findings":[{"checkId":"core/doctor/ui-protocol-freshness","severity":"warning","message":"UI assets are older than the protocol schema.\nFunctional changes since last build:\n- ef7e652ec4 test(codex): avoid forced-tool allowlist flake","path":"/redacted/openclaw/dist/control-ui/index.html","fixHint":"Run `openclaw doctor --fix` to build Control UI assets."}]}
--- stale assets dry-run repair result ---

```

Observed result after fix:
The structured health check reports missing Control UI assets and stale assets through lint JSON. Dry-run repair returns preview process effects for build/rebuild without generated file diffs. Real doctor --fix repair remains in the existing pre-health UI freshness slot, while the broad structured runner excludes positional-owned checks. When git can prove there are no schema commits since the UI build, no stale finding is emitted; when git history is unavailable, the mtime mismatch remains reportable without commit details. Slim/source-limited installs do not get prompted to run an impossible UI rebuild.

What was not tested:
No live Control UI rebuild was run because this PR intentionally leaves build/rebuild execution on the existing doctor preflight repair path. No generated UI asset diff is emitted because generated build output is not stable or cheap to preview as a file diff.

commands.review_summary:
The PR adds and reuses shared coercion, list-normalization, dedupe, and primitive helpers across core, Plugin SDK, UI, channels, providers, and bundled plugins.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as an issue reproduction; this is a PR review. The blocking concerns are source-reproducible from the diff: public SDK export expansion, Google Meet record coercion broadening, memory extraPaths stringification, and locale-dependent shared sorting.
commands.review_followup: Manual review is needed because the remaining blockers include public SDK direction and contributor real behavior proof that repair automation cannot supply.

target.title: refactor: reuse shared coercion helpers
target.body:
## Summary

- Replace repeated trim/drop-empty/dedupe string-list code with shared helpers, including order-preserving `uniqueStrings`, unique normalized entries, and unique trimmed-list variants.
- Reuse the helpers across core CLI, gateway, agents, plugin registry/setup, model catalog, doctor repairs, hooks, PATH handling, receipts, proxy capture, policy surfaces, Codex native search config, plugin SDK channel streaming, media-understanding text extraction, message-access, media URL, install path, update-runner, auth sidecar paths, runtime plugin IDs, pairing allow-from storage, command-risk summaries, and extension provider/channel surfaces.
- Keep the existing modern-helper refactors and AGENTS readability guidance from the earlier PR stack, while preserving the browser-safe command registry import boundary.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.json --noEmit --pretty false`

This PR adds a bundled opt-in GitHub Copilot agent runtime plugin, docs and QA inventory, on-demand SDK install support, dependency/lockfile updates, build/labeler wiring, and focused runtime tests.

PR surface: Source +5501, Tests +9085, Docs +792, Config +75, Other +157. Total +15610 across 62 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review blockers: source inspection shows prepack runs pnpm build while build-all does not copy the Copilot install manifest, and installCopilotSdk skips solely on SDK directory existence. target.title: [AI] feat(copilot): add GitHub Copilot agent runtime
target.body:
## Round 2 review fixes (2026-05-24)

Addressed all three reviewer findings on the previous head `d81505c539`: P1 ΓÇö bridged tools now honor the resolved sandbox context (caller-side `resolveSandboxContext` in `attempt.ts` plus `sandbox`/`spawnWorkspaceDir` forwarded through `tool-bridge.ts`); P2 ΓÇö `@github/copilot-sdk` is now an optional `peerDependency` with an on-demand install (~260 MB) wired into the wizard via `src/commands/copilot-sdk-install.ts` and a typed dynamic loader in `extensions/copilot/src/sdk-loader.ts`; and the unrelated `plugin: meeting-notes` labeler block is restored in `.github/labeler.yml`. Validated locally with `tsgo:core` + `tsgo:extensions` clean, `check:import-cycles` 0, `extensions/copilot` 19 files / 424 tests passing (was 18/409 before the fixes), 14 new tests in `src/commands/copilot-sdk-install.test.ts`, 4 in `src/agents/copilot-routing.test.ts`, and a live gateway chat-completion smoke against `github-copilot/gpt-4.1` returning the literal expected reply with full prompt-token usage (22,469 tokens including the bootstrap-files context).

## Summary

Adds a bundled `copilot` plugin that registers an opt-in **agent runtime**
backed by [`@github/copilot-sdk`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@github/copilot-sdk)
and the GitHub Copilot CLI. Selection is fully **config-driven**. The runtime registers via
  `api.registerAgentHarness({ id: "copilot", ... })` and is selected by
  setting `agentRuntime.id: "copilot"` on a model or provider entry.

- What changed:
  - New bundled plugin `extensions/copilot/` (harness + 13 bridges +
    doctor probes + tests).
  - Workspace bootstrap support so the Copilot agent receives
    `IDENTITY.md`, `SOUL.md`, and `HEARTBEAT.md` (parity with the
    bundled PI / Codex runtimes).
  - Docs: new `docs/plugins/copilot.md`; additions to
    `docs/concepts/agent-runtimes.md` and `docs/concepts/models.md` so
    the selection rules are discoverable.
  - QA capability inventory `qa/copilot-capabilities.md` pinning the
    runtime against `@github/copilot-sdk@1.0.0-beta.4`.
  - `.github/labeler.yml` entry for `extensions: copilot`.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary):
  - **No source-tree changes outside `extensions/copilot/` and docs/QA.**

## Motivation

OpenClaw users currently can't run any OpenClaw agent using the GitHub
Copilot SDK as the agent runtime. This change adds the copilot agent
runtime as a peer to `pi` / `codex` so the same agent definition can be
flipped between runtimes via one config field, while PI remains the safe
default for everyone else.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Feature
- [x] Docs

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Integrations

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: route an agent attempt through the GitHub
  Copilot SDK runtime instead of PI, opt-in per model or per provider.
- Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu (Linux), OpenClaw built from this
  branch, gateway running locally against a real GitHub Copilot
  subscription token.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

  ```jsonc
  // ~/.openclaw-copilot-play/openclaw.json
  {
    "agents": {
      "defaults": {
        "model": { "primary": "github-copilot/gpt-4.1" },
        "models": {
          "github-copilot/gpt-4.1": { "agentRuntime": { "id": "copilot" } }
        }
      }
    },
    "plugins": { "entries": { "copilot": { "enabled": true } } },
    "gateway": {
      "mode": "local",
      "http": {
        "endpoints": {
          "chatCompletions": { "enabled": true },
          "responses":       { "enabled": true }
        }
      }
    }
  }
  ```

  ```bash
  corepack pnpm install
  corepack pnpm build
  OPENCLAW_GITHUB_TOKEN=<redacted> \
    node openclaw.mjs --profile copilot-sdk-play gateway run \
      --port 18790 --bind loopback \
      --auth token --token <redacted>

  curl -sS -X POST \
    -H 'Authorization: Bearer <redacted>' \
    -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
    http://127.0.0.1:18790/v1/chat/completions \
    --data '{"model":"openclaw/main","stream":false,
             "messages":[{"role":"user",
               "content":"reply with only POST-UPSTREAM-CLEAN-PORT-OK"}]}'
  ```

- Evidence after fix (gateway log + HTTP response, redacted):

  ```
  [gateway] auto-enabled plugins for this runtime without writing config:
    - github-copilot/gpt-4.1 model configured, enabled automatically.
  [gateway] agent model: github-copilot/gpt-4.1 (thinking=medium, fast=off)
  [gateway] http server listening
    (9 plugins: acpx, browser, canvas, copilot, device-pair,
     file-transfer, memory-core, phone-control, talk-voice; 0.9s)
  [gateway] ready
  ```

  ```json
  {
    "id": "chatcmpl_92b6e47c-e067-46bf-ad7d-45989f6dd288",
    "object": "chat.completion",
    "model": "openclaw/main",
    "choices": [{
      "index": 0,
      "message": { "role": "assistant",
                   "content": "POST-UPSTREAM-CLEAN-PORT-OK" },
      "finish_reason": "stop"
    }],
    "usage": { "prompt_tokens": 22456,
               "completion_tokens": 11,
               "total_tokens": 22467 }
  }
  ```

- Observed result after fix: the gateway loads the `copilot` plugin
  alongside the other bundled plugins; the agent attempt is dispatched
  through the `copilot` runtime; the reply comes back through the
  Copilot SDK transport. Removing `agentRuntime.id` falls back to PI
  with no other change.
- What was not tested: BYOK adapters (explicitly out of scope — the runtime is
  hard-bound to the canonical `github-copilot` provider).
- Before evidence: with `plugins.entries.copilot.enabled` false (or
  absent), the `agentRuntime.id: "copilot"` selector resolves to
  nothing and the attempt falls back to PI via the existing
  `selectAgentHarness` path — no other behaviour change for hosts that
  don't opt in.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A — feature add.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A — no bug.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New opt-in agent runtime id `copilot`, selectable via
  `agentRuntime.id` on a model entry or `runtime.id` on a provider
  entry.
- New bundled plugin entry `plugins.entries.copilot.enabled`. When the
  entry is disabled or absent, the runtime id is unknown to the
  registry and the resolver falls back to PI with no behaviour change
  for existing users.
- `auto` selection never picks `copilot` — selection is always
  explicit.
- `/btw` for a `github-copilot/*`-model agent under the `copilot`
  runtime uses the same in-tree PI fallback path
  (`src/agents/btw.ts` â†’ `streamSimple` via the `github-copilot`
  provider) that any other runtime would. The runtime does not
  implement `runSideQuestion`, so there is no SDK-specific `/btw`
  divergence to maintain.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[agent attempt] -> [PI runtime] -> [Copilot HTTP via the
                                    github-copilot provider]

After (only when agentRuntime.id === "copilot"):
[agent attempt] -> [copilot runtime] -> [@github/copilot-sdk +
                                          GitHub Copilot CLI]

Otherwise unchanged:
[agent attempt] -> [PI runtime] -> [unchanged path]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No` — the runtime reads tokens the
  same way the rest of OpenClaw does (`GITHUB_TOKEN` /
  `OPENCLAW_GITHUB_TOKEN` / the existing `github-copilot:` auth-profile
  prefix / the logged-in Copilot CLI user). No new env vars are read
  and no new auth-profile namespace is added.
- New/changed network calls? `Yes (additive)` — outbound HTTPS to the
  Copilot endpoints the bundled SDK targets (same surface that
  subscription Copilot users already hit via
  `extensions/github-copilot/`). No new third-party hosts.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `Yes (additive)` — the
  runtime can invoke the local `copilot` CLI via `@github/copilot-sdk`
  for SDK transport. `probeCopilotCliVersion` in
  `extensions/copilot/src/doctor-probes.ts` documents and gates this in
  `openclaw doctor`.
- Data access scope changed? `No`. Per-agent `copilotHome` keeps
  Copilot CLI state isolated under
  `~/.openclaw/agents/<agentId>/copilot` by default.
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: see Risks / Mitigations
  below.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: WSL Ubuntu (Linux); the build itself also succeeds on Windows.
- Runtime/container: Node 22 via Corepack / pnpm.
- Model/provider: `github-copilot/gpt-4.1` from the bundled
  `extensions/github-copilot` provider catalog.
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A — gateway running locally
  (`gateway.mode: "local"`, chat-completions HTTP endpoint enabled).
- Relevant config (redacted): see Real behavior proof above.

### Steps

1. `corepack pnpm install && corepack pnpm build`.
2. Point `OPENCLAW_HOME` (or `--profile`) at a profile with the
   snippet above.
3. Run the `gateway run` command and the `curl` POST shown above.

### Expected

- `runtime-plugins` stage loads the bundled `copilot` plugin.
- The runtime's `supports({ provider: "github-copilot",
  requestedRuntime: "copilot" })` returns `{ supported: true,
  priority: 100 }`.
- Attempt dispatched through `runCopilotAttempt`; reply returned by
  the Copilot SDK transport.
- Gateway returns the OpenAI-shaped chat-completion response with the
  literal answer.

### Actual

- Matches expected. Removing `agentRuntime.id` (or disabling the
  plugin entry) reverts to PI for the same model with no other change.

## Evidence

Repeatable from a fresh clone of `openclaw/openclaw` with this branch
checked out on top of upstream `main`:

```bash
corepack pnpm install
# (clean install)

corepack pnpm check:import-cycles
# Import cycle check: 0 runtime value cycle(s).

corepack pnpm tsgo:core
# (no errors)

corepack pnpm tsgo:extensions
# (no errors)

node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/copilot
# Test Files  19 passed (19)
#       Tests  424 passed (424)

node scripts/test-projects.mjs \
  src/agents/btw.test.ts \
  src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts \
  src/agents/harness/registry.test.ts \
  src/agents/bootstrap-files.test.ts
# Test Files  4 passed (4)
#       Tests  108 passed (108)

corepack pnpm build
# build-all completed (dist/extensions/copilot/ produced)
```

Live gateway smoke (redacted token), already shown under
"Real behavior proof":

```
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
{"choices":[{"message":{"content":"POST-UPSTREAM-CLEAN-PORT-OK"}, ...}], ...}
```

### Sandbox-enabled live verification (P1 end-to-end proof)

Beyond the unit tests for `resolveSandboxContext` wiring, I stood up
a real Docker sandbox in WSL Ubuntu 24.04 and ran the bundled Copilot
runtime against it. Profile config (`~/.openclaw-copilot-sdk-play/openclaw.json`):

```json
"agents": {
  "defaults": {
    "model": { "primary": "github-copilot/gpt-4.1" },
    "models": {
      "github-copilot/gpt-4.1": { "agentRuntime": { "id": "copilot" } }
    },
    "sandbox": {
      "mode": "all",
      "backend": "docker",
      "workspaceAccess": "rw",
      "docker": { "image": "openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim" }
    }
  }
}
```

Built the default sandbox image via `bash scripts/sandbox-setup.sh`,
restarted the gateway, sent a chat completion asking the agent to
write a file. Gateway log shows the sandbox config was loaded and the
tool policy applied:

```
[agents/tool-policy] tool policy removed 3 tool(s) via sandbox tools.deny: cron, gateway, nodes
[agents/tool-policy] tool policy removed 5 tool(s) via sandbox tools.allow: agents_list, message, tts, web_fetch, web_search
```

Docker container created from our config and bind-mounted to the
agent's workspace:

```
$ docker ps --filter ancestor=openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim
openclaw-sbx-agent-main-f331f052  Up About a minute  openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim  "sleep infinity"

$ docker inspect openclaw-sbx-agent-main-f331f052 --format '{{json .Mounts}}'
[{ "Type":"bind",
   "Source":"/root/.openclaw/workspace-copilot-sdk-play",
   "Destination":"/workspace",
   "Mode":"z", "RW":true, "Propagation":"rprivate" }]
```

The bridged file-write tool (now receiving the resolved sandbox
context after the P1 fix) wrote into the container's `/workspace`
rather than to the host:

```
$ docker exec openclaw-sbx-agent-main-f331f052 cat /workspace/SANDBOX-PROOF.txt
HELLO-FROM-SANDBOX-PR-86155

$ cat /root/.openclaw/workspace-copilot-sdk-play/SANDBOX-PROOF.txt
HELLO-FROM-SANDBOX-PR-86155
```

The host file is present because the workspace bind-mount is `rw`
(matching the configured `workspaceAccess`). Without the P1 fix the
bridged tools would have received `sandbox: undefined` and written
straight to the host workspace, bypassing the container entirely.

### On-demand install transcript (P2 end-to-end proof)

Cleared `~/.openclaw/npm-runtime/copilot/` and invoked the install
helper directly (the same function the wizard hook calls):

```
$ rm -rf /root/.openclaw/npm-runtime/copilot && \
    node_modules/.bin/tsx -e \
    'import { installCopilotSdk, isCopilotSdkInstalled, COPILOT_SDK_FALLBACK_DIR, COPILOT_SDK_SPEC } \
       from "./src/commands/copilot-sdk-install.ts"; \
     console.log(`before: ${isCopilotSdkInstalled()}`); \
     await installCopilotSdk({ logger: console.log }); \
     console.log(`after:  ${isCopilotSdkInstalled()}`);'

before: false
[copilot] installing @github/copilot-sdk@1.0.0-beta.4 into /root/.openclaw/npm-runtime/copilot ...
added 6 packages in 3s
[copilot] installed @github/copilot-sdk@1.0.0-beta.4
after:  true
```

The same install fires from the wizard the first time a user selects
a `github-copilot/*` model and confirms the prompt (see
`src/wizard/select-model-runtime.ts`).

Required-evidence checklist:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after — unit + targeted vitest
      pass for `extensions/copilot` (19 files / 424 tests) and the
      core agent surface (4 files / 108 tests). Before this branch, the
      plugin does not exist, so the dedicated tests don't run; the
      core-agent suite is unchanged at the file level vs `origin/main`
      apart from the new `bootstrap-files.test.ts`, which exercises the
      shared bootstrap path the new runtime opts into.
- [x] Trace/log snippets — gateway log + chat-completions response
      above.
- [ ] Screenshot/recording — N/A (CLI / HTTP).
- [ ] Perf numbers — N/A (no perf claim).

## Human Verification (required)

What I personally verified (not just CI):

- Verified scenarios:
  - `corepack pnpm check:import-cycles` â†’ `0 cycle(s)`.
  - `corepack pnpm tsgo:core` clean.
  - `corepack pnpm tsgo:extensions` clean.
  - `node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/copilot` â†’ 424/424.
  - `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/btw.test.ts
    src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts
    src/agents/harness/registry.test.ts
    src/agents/bootstrap-files.test.ts` â†’ 108/108.
  - `corepack pnpm build` produced `dist/extensions/copilot/`.
  - Live dogfood: `github-copilot/gpt-4.1` with
    `agentRuntime.id: "copilot"` returns a real assistant turn through
    the local gateway. Same config with `agentRuntime` removed routes
    back through PI with no other change.
  - Workspace bootstrap dogfood: with `IDENTITY.md`, `SOUL.md`, and
    `HEARTBEAT.md` in the workspace, the Copilot agent receives and
    references their contents (parity with PI / Codex).
- Edge cases checked:
  - Runtime selection: `supports()` rejects any provider other than
    `github-copilot`.
  - Runtime selection: `supports()` returns `{ supported: false }`
    (not `auto`) when `requestedRuntime` is unset, so `auto` never
    lands here by accident.
  - Plugin disabled / absent â†’ the runtime id is unknown to the
    registry, `selectAgentHarness` falls back to PI, no crash.
  - `/btw` on a `copilot`-runtime agent goes through the in-tree PI
    fallback (`btw.ts` â†’ `streamSimple` via the `github-copilot`
    provider), exercising the same path the codebase has used for
    every `github-copilot/*`-model agent on `origin/main` for a long
    time. The runtime deliberately does not implement
    `runSideQuestion`; a regression that re-introduces it lights up
    in `extensions/copilot/harness.test.ts`.
- What I did **not** verify:
  - Any non-`github-copilot` provider routing through this runtime —
    explicitly out of scope.
  - TUI surfaces — the runtime addition is not related to TUI;

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes` — pure additive. No existing config field
  changes meaning. Without `agentRuntime.id: "copilot"` (or with the
  plugin entry disabled / absent), the new runtime is dormant.
- Config/env changes? `No new env`. One new optional plugin entry
  `plugins.entries.copilot` with a tiny `configSchema` (only
  `pool.idleTtlMs`).
- Migration needed? `No`.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: bundling vs third-party policy — `CONTRIBUTING` notes that most
  features should ship as third-party plugins.
  - Mitigation: this mirrors the precedent set by `extensions/codex/`
    (bundled, first-party agent runtime). `publishToNpm: true` is set
    on `openclaw.plugin.json`, so flipping it out-of-tree is a
    single-flag decision for maintainers.

## Note for maintainers

This is an **AI-assisted** PR. `CHANGELOG.md` is intentionally left
untouched per `AGENTS.md` / ClawSweeper. Suggested changelog entry on
land:

> Agent runtimes: add the bundled `copilot` plugin as a peer agent
> runtime backed by `@github/copilot-sdk` and the GitHub Copilot CLI,
> selectable per-model or per-provider via
> `agentRuntime.id: "copilot"`; the canonical `github-copilot`
> provider is claimed by the runtime's doctor contract. PI remains the
> default and `auto` never selects `copilot`.

target.title: Redesign Overview as an operator-first OpenClaw dashboard
target.body:
## Summary
- Rework the connected Overview page into an operator-first dashboard instead of a setup/status landing page.
- Keep first-run Gateway Access and Snapshot cards intact for disconnected installs, then move them below daily operating signals once the Control UI is connected.
- Use existing gateway, session, cron, auth, provider/quota, connector, skill, and log data. This PR intentionally avoids adding new Gateway RPCs.

## Major functional improvements
- Makes the Overview useful at a glance: the first screen now answers what needs attention, what just ran, whether model/provider usage looks healthy, and whether cron/connectors/skills/logs look normal.
- Reduces dashboard scrolling: Attention and Recent Sessions stop growing without bound, and the usage/quota surface now uses a compact metric layout instead of a tall card.
- Respects OpenClaw's multi-model posture: the dashboard presents provider/quota health from the data available in the install, without implying OpenClaw is tied to one LLM provider.
- Shows clearer provider-limit detail when providers expose different kinds of limits: time buckets, model-tier buckets, token budgets, credit balances, and monthly quotas use provider-reported labels instead of a generic "quota" tile.
- Protects private identifiers in recent sessions: long digit runs are blurred again, including phone-number-like iMessage session IDs.
- Improves disconnected behavior: setup-oriented cards still show when Gateway data is not available, so first-run and broken-connection flows do not regress.
- Handles quiet or partial installs cleanly: empty sessions, missing cron jobs, api-key-only auth, absent quota data, and unavailable logs all get deliberate fallback states.

## Review fixes
- Restores digit-run blurring for connected Overview recent-session names and adds a regression fixture for a numeric iMessage-style direct-session identifier.
- Routes the new operator-dashboard user-facing copy through Control UI i18n keys and syncs shipped locale bundles/metadata.
- Replaces the Codex-specific usage card title with provider/model usage wording while still showing concrete provider names and provider-reported quota labels when providers supply usage data.
- Uses quota data from any provider that reports it, including API-key providers, while keeping the separate auth-health card focused on OAuth/token providers that can expire. Self-describing labels like OpenRouter `Credits` are no longer forced into `Credits quota`.
- Applies the same provider-native quota formatting to the chat header pill, so OpenRouter-style `Credits` renders with native remaining labels such as `$12.34 left` instead of `Credits quota` plus a normalized percentage.
- Adds provider-native quota display labels to the shared usage model, so balance providers can show dollar/credit amounts while percentage fields still support sorting and pressure ranking.
- Adds OpenRouter usage fetching for `/api/v1/credits` and `/api/v1/key`, displaying account credits/key limits as dollar balances when OpenRouter returns them.
- Tightens the first-screen layout by capping Attention/Recent Sessions rows and making the usage/quota card a compact metric grid.

## Screenshots
Redacted connected Overview screenshot from the current branch UI. This local Gateway capture currently returned no provider quota limits, so it shows the deliberate no-quota fallback state:

![Redacted connected OpenClaw Overview dashboard screenshot](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/homer-byte/openclaw/pr-assets/86460/.github/pr-assets/86460/openclaw-overview-pr-proof-redacted.png)

Redacted provider-quota fixture showing an OpenRouter-style dollar credits balance alongside a Codex 5h reset and a Z.ai token window. This fixture illustrates provider-shaped quota rendering without claiming the local install had live OpenRouter quota data at capture time:

![Redacted OpenRouter-style provider quota fixture screenshot](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/homer-byte/openclaw/pr-assets/86460/.github/pr-assets/86460/openclaw-overview-openrouter-quota-redacted.png)

## Validation
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/infra/provider-usage.format.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.shared.test.ts extensions/openrouter/index.test.ts extensions/openrouter/usage.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/overview.render.test.ts`
- `pnpm ui:i18n:sync`
- `pnpm ui:i18n:check`
- `pnpm format:docs:check`
- `pnpm ui:build`
- `pnpm lint:ui:no-raw-window-open`
- `pnpm build`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Connected Control UI Overview should show the compact operator dashboard while preserving privacy for recent-session identifiers, avoiding unbounded first-screen card height, and presenting model/provider usage without making OpenClaw look Codex-only or forcing credit/balance providers into percentage-only labels.
- **Real environment tested:** Local working OpenClaw Gateway at `http://127.0.0.1:18789/`, with the current branch Control UI bundle served locally for capture. The running installed Gateway was older than the branch, so provider usage was also verified directly through the current branch usage loader.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Built the repo, ran focused Overview/chat render tests plus i18n/build/diff checks, opened Overview with Playwright against the local Gateway, redacted private text in the DOM, and captured screenshots. A second redacted fixture patches the visible provider-usage panel to illustrate OpenRouter dollar-credit display alongside a Codex reset window and a Z.ai token window.
- **Evidence after fix:** The current branch provider-usage loader returned real Codex windows:

```json
{
  "provider": "openai-codex",
  "displayName": "Codex",
  "windows": [
    { "label": "5h", "usedPercent": 10 },
    { "label": "Week", "usedPercent": 15 }
  ]
}
```

The refreshed connected Overview screenshot rendered the no-quota fallback when the local Gateway returned no provider limits:

```text
QUOTA
unavailable
Model auth loaded, but no provider quota limits were returned; showing local session usage.
```

The OpenRouter/Codex render fixture now shows dollar remaining labels and a Codex reset row without repeating that reset in a footer:

```text
CREDITS
12%
OpenRouter · Credits    $12.34 left
Codex · 5h              reset 2h 9m   15% left
Z.ai · Tokens (6h)      reset 2h 59m  60% left
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The connected Overview rendered the operator dashboard and the provider-quota fallback matched the local Gateway data. The OpenRouter/Codex fixture demonstrates provider-native dollar balances and Codex reset rows while retaining normalized percentages for ranking and for providers whose APIs report percentage/count windows. The redundant prose footer is suppressed when provider quota rows already carry the provider/limit/reset details.
- **What was not tested:** No live OpenRouter account balance was queried on the local install; the OpenRouter screenshot remains a labeled fixture. Broader cross-browser visual QA remains ordinary maintainer review scope.

## Implementation notes
- This PR intentionally avoids new Gateway RPCs. Message queue state, update posture, and host-health exceptions are represented only when existing dashboard data can support them.
- Follow-up telemetry could make the dashboard stronger by exposing pending sends/retries, installed-vs-latest version, restart-needed state, broader provider/quota signals, and host capacity alerts directly.

target.title: feat: expose model pricing to plugins via runtime.usage API
target.body:
## Summary
Adds a `runtime.usage` namespace to the plugin runtime, exposing `resolveModelCostConfig()` and `estimateUsageCost()` so context engine plugins can calculate model-cost estimates from OpenClaw's configured pricing instead of hardcoded heuristics.

## Motivation
Context engine plugins, such as [ContextClaw](https://github.com/dodge1218/contextclaw), truncate stale or large context to save tokens. Today they can only estimate savings with a fixed heuristic. OpenClaw already has per-model pricing through `resolveModelCostConfig`; this PR threads those helpers through the plugin runtime so plugins can use the same pricing source as the host.

## Changes
- `src/plugins/runtime/runtime-usage.ts` — adds the runtime usage facade.
- `src/plugins/runtime/types-core.ts` — adds the `usage` namespace to `PluginRuntimeCore`.
- `src/plugins/runtime/index.ts` — wires `createRuntimeUsage()` into runtime assembly.
- `src/plugin-sdk/test-helpers/plugin-runtime-mock.ts` — updates the plugin runtime mock.
- `src/plugins/runtime/index.test.ts` — asserts the runtime exposes both usage helpers.
- `docs/plugins/sdk-runtime.md` — documents the plugin SDK usage helpers.
- `docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256` — regenerates the public SDK API baseline.

## Real Behavior Proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** A plugin can access the new `api.runtime.usage` namespace and call both pricing helpers without importing OpenClaw internals.

- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on branch `feat/plugin-cost-api` at `eb8400c3a3`, after rebasing onto upstream `main` and narrowing the PR to the plugin runtime/docs/test surface.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
pnpm exec tsx .artifacts/pr64436-runtime-usage-proof/run-direct-plugin-api-proof.ts
```

- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from a minimal external plugin fixture with `openclaw.plugin.json` plus `index.mjs`; its `register(api)` calls `api.runtime.usage.resolveModelCostConfig(...)` and `api.runtime.usage.estimateUsageCost(...)`.

```text
[proof] repoHead=eb8400c3a3
[proof] pluginDir=/tmp/openclaw-runtime-usage-direct-foU7Uy
[proof] manifest=/tmp/openclaw-runtime-usage-direct-foU7Uy/openclaw.plugin.json
[plugin] id=runtime-usage-proof
[plugin] hasRuntimeUsage=true
[plugin] resolveModelCostConfig cost={"input":3,"output":15,"cacheRead":0.3,"cacheWrite":3.75}
[plugin] estimateUsageCost total=0.010950
[proof] PASS plugin register(api) called both api.runtime.usage helpers
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The plugin saw `api.runtime.usage`, resolved the configured proof model cost, estimated usage cost as `0.010950`, and exited with the PASS line above.

- **What was not tested:** Full OpenClaw app startup and provider billing reconciliation; this proof is scoped to the plugin runtime API surface added by this PR.

## Local Verification
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`
- `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 node --import tsx scripts/generate-plugin-sdk-api-baseline.ts --check`
- `pnpm format:check docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256 docs/plugins/sdk-runtime.md src/plugins/runtime/index.test.ts src/plugins/runtime/index.ts src/plugins/runtime/types-core.ts src/plugins/runtime/runtime-usage.ts src/plugin-sdk/test-helpers/plugin-runtime-mock.ts`
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugins/runtime/index.test.ts -t 'exposes runtime.usage pricing helpers' --reporter=dot`

## Related
- Issue #64085 — Provider circuit breaker cost/status reporting.
- PR #64127 — companion status-provider work.

target.title: Expose plugin approval action metadata
target.body:
## Review context Concrete plugin: [Guardiola31337/openclaw-agentkit](https://github.com/Guardiola31337/openclaw-agentkit). The intent is to keep World ID / AgentKit out of OpenClaw core while letting external HITL plugins use the existing approval flow end-to-end. This PR's role: approval action metadata and no-route pending surface; it does not resolve verified approvals by itself. Bundle: - [#82431](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/82431) exposes approval action metadata/UI rendering. - [#82434](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/82434) adds verified approval resolution with plugin ownership checks. - [#82471](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/82471) adds chat/status metadata and idempotency for durable approval updates. ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Agent participant Gateway participant Plugin as HITL plugin participant UI as TUI/Web UI participant Verifier as External verifier Agent->>Gateway: protected tool call Gateway->>Plugin: approval hook/request Gateway->>UI: approval card + plugin actions UI->>Plugin: user chooses verify once / trust session / deny Plugin->>Verifier: external proof flow Verifier-->>Plugin: verified result Plugin->>Gateway: resolve verified approval Gateway->>Agent: allow/deny blocked tool Plugin->>UI: status/update messages with idempotency ``` ```mermaid flowchart LR A["Approval action surface
#82431"] --> B["Verified resolver
#82434"] B --> C["Status/chat idempotency
#82471"] C --> D["Usable external HITL plugin flow"] ``` Trust boundary to review: should a plugin that already owns a pending approval be allowed to complete that specific approval after external verification, through a typed SDK/Gateway path with admin scope and ownership checks? Summary: - Add plugin approval action templates to the before_tool_call approval result and plugin.approval.request schema. - Expand templates with the server-generated approval id, store the resulting action commands, and carry them through reply payloads and approval view models. - Add keepPendingWithoutRoute so external HITL plugins can keep plugin approvals pending when no core approval route/client is active. - Keep native decision-button channel adapters compatible with command-only approval actions. - Update the generated Swift protocol model and plugin SDK API baseline for the intentional additive surface change. Verification: - `pnpm protocol:check` - `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check` - `pnpm tsgo:extensions` - `pnpm build:plugin-sdk:strict-smoke` - `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/discord/src/approval-handler.runtime.ts extensions/matrix/src/approval-handler.runtime.ts extensions/qqbot/src/bridge/approval/handler-runtime.ts extensions/telegram/src/approval-handler.runtime.ts` - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-approval.test.ts src/infra/approval-view-model.test.ts src/infra/exec-approval-reply.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/approval-renderers.test.ts extensions/discord/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts extensions/matrix/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/bridge/approval/handler-runtime.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/approval/index.test.ts src/agents/pi-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts src/plugins/hooks.before-tool-call.test.ts` - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts` - `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` ## Real behavior proof Behavior addressed: external plugins can attach approval-specific command actions to plugin approval requests and keep those requests pending while their own HITL command flow is responsible for resolution. Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, rebased onto current `origin/main` on May 16, 2026. Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran the protocol generator/check, plugin SDK API baseline check, extension typecheck, plugin SDK strict declaration smoke, targeted extension oxlint, focused approval/gateway/plugin Vitest coverage, the host hook contract test, and whitespace diff check. Evidence after fix: ```text $ pnpm protocol:check wrote dist/protocol.schema.json wrote apps/shared/OpenClawKit/Sources/OpenClawProtocol/GatewayModels.swift $ pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check OK docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256 $ pnpm tsgo:extensions (exit 0) $ pnpm build:plugin-sdk:strict-smoke (exit 0) $ node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/discord/src/approval-handler.runtime.ts extensions/matrix/src/approval-handler.runtime.ts extensions/qqbot/src/bridge/approval/handler-runtime.ts extensions/telegram/src/approval-handler.runtime.ts Found 0 warnings and 0 errors. $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-approval.test.ts src/infra/approval-view-model.test.ts src/infra/exec-approval-reply.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/approval-renderers.test.ts extensions/discord/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts extensions/matrix/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/bridge/approval/handler-runtime.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/approval/index.test.ts src/agents/pi-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts src/plugins/hooks.before-tool-call.test.ts Test Files 10 passed (10) Tests 122 passed (122) $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts Test Files 1 passed (1) Tests 50 passed (50) $ git diff --check origin/main...HEAD (no output) ``` Observed result after fix: the protocol and SDK baselines reflect the new additive action metadata, representative adapters preserve command actions, approval view/reply rendering carries those commands, and the before_tool_call host hook contract remains compatible. What was not tested: full release suite, live World ID callback flow, and the follow-up verified plugin-owned resolution API that is split into #82434. Refs #82336 ## External plugin integration proof Behavior addressed: the external ClawHub AgentKit plugin can consume the combined temporary OpenClaw host API branch that includes this PR's approval actions and no-route pending behavior. Real environment tested: GitHub Actions in `Guardiola31337/openclaw-agentkit` on May 16, 2026, checking out `Guardiola31337/openclaw@agentkit/external-plugin-host-apis` and linking it into the external plugin package. Exact steps or command run after this patch: the remote workflow checked out the temporary OpenClaw host branch, installed with frozen lockfiles, ran `pnpm build gatewayWatch`, ran `pnpm build:plugin-sdk:strict-smoke`, linked the host checkout into the AgentKit plugin, then ran `pnpm test:hitl` and `pnpm pack:check`. Evidence after fix: ```text $ gh run watch 25972624778 --repo Guardiola31337/openclaw-agentkit --exit-status ✓ main CI · 25972624778 ✓ build in 1m46s ✓ Run pnpm build gatewayWatch ✓ Run pnpm build:plugin-sdk:strict-smoke ✓ Run pnpm dev:link-openclaw .openclaw-source ✓ Run pnpm test:hitl ✓ Run pnpm pack:check ``` Observed result after fix: the external plugin builds against the split OpenClaw host APIs, the plugin SDK declarations expose the needed approval/action surface, the AgentKit HITL proof passes, and the package dry-run includes the expected ClawHub plugin artifact. What was not tested: production World ID credential verification. This remote proof exercises the integrated temporary host branch because the external plugin requires this PR together with #82434 and #82471 for the full HITL path.

target.title: Doctor: expose UI freshness health findings
target.body:
## Summary

- Exposes Control UI protocol freshness as a structured doctor health check for lint and dry-run repair effects.
- Keeps real build/rebuild mutation on the existing `maybeRepairUiProtocolFreshness()` path used by `openclaw doctor --fix`.
- Keeps existing doctor repairs in their positional slots: the pre-health UI freshness step still calls the legacy repair path, and the broad structured runner now excludes positional-owned health checks.
- Hardens the stale detector so a successful empty git history does not create false stale findings, while unavailable git history still reports the mtime mismatch without commit details.
- Skips build/rebuild prompts when the checkout does not contain `ui/` sources.

This PR does not change the public health/plugin SDK contract, does not invent file diffs for generated UI assets, and does not move existing doctor repair order into the broad structured runner.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
`openclaw doctor --lint` can report missing/stale Control UI assets, and dry-run repair output can preview the build/rebuild process effect without mutating generated UI files or changing the existing `openclaw doctor --fix` repair ownership.

Real environment tested:
WSL Ubuntu 24.04 durable OpenClaw worktree at `/root/src/openclaw-ui-freshness-stack`, head `f2a6b4af48`, source harness using the real doctor health registry and temporary ignored `dist/control-ui/index.html` states. Paths below are redacted.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. `git diff --check`
2. `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/commands/doctor-ui.ts src/commands/doctor-ui.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-conversion-plan.ts`
3. `node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/commands/doctor-ui.ts src/commands/doctor-ui.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-conversion-plan.ts`

target.title: fix(agents): wait for compaction before requester steering fallback
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #86566 (follow-up to #85489). Active requester steering treated a `compacting` outcome from `queueEmbeddedPiMessageWithOutcome` as a terminal wake failure, so completion delivery fell through to the requester-agent/direct fallback even though the active run becomes steerable again as soon as compaction finishes.

This introduces a shared `resolveActiveWakeWithRetries` helper used by **both** active-wake call sites — the steer path (`maybeSteerSubagentAnnounce`) and the generated-completion active wake (`sendSubagentAnnounceDirectly`). The helper treats `compacting` as transient: it waits through compaction and retries the same wake, bounded by cancellation and a finite backoff schedule that stays well within the announce delivery timeout. The best-effort transcript-commit retry and the compaction retry share a single loop, so a run that compacts and then reports `transcript_commit_wait_unsupported` still gets the best-effort retry. Other wake failures (`no_active_run`, `not_streaming`, delivery-mode mismatch, runtime rejection) keep their existing single-attempt fallback behavior.

Addresses the earlier review notes: the compaction retry now also covers the completion wake path, and the best-effort fallback is preserved after compaction by handling both transient retries in one loop.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: when an active requester is compacting and compaction finishes within the delivery timeout, both the steer path and the generated-completion wake should wait and re-steer the same active run instead of falling back to direct requester-agent delivery.

Real environment tested: Linux (Ubuntu 24.04, WSL2), Node.js 24.14.0, current PR head. Exercised the real `maybeSteerSubagentAnnounce` and `sendSubagentAnnounceDirectly` paths through `deliverSubagentAnnouncement` under the repo's vitest runner, with no mocking of the module under test (only the queue-outcome dependency is driven to simulate the compacting-then-ready sequence). Compared the pre-fix and post-fix code.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran the subagent announce delivery suite, including regression tests that drive the steer queue and the completion-handoff wake to return `compacting` first and a queued success second, plus a guard test that a non-compacting failure is not retried.

Evidence after fix: stdout from the vitest run against the real steering and completion paths, comparing pre-fix and post-fix:
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts -t 86566
-- BEFORE the fix (compacting is terminal on both paths): regression tests fail --
x waits through compaction and re-steers the active requester (86566)
AssertionError: expected 'direct' to deeply equal 'steered'
x waits through compaction on the completion handoff wake (86566)
AssertionError: expected 'direct' to deeply equal 'steered'

does not retry non-compacting steer failures (86566)
Tests  4 failed | 2 passed | 126 skipped (132)

-- AFTER the fix (compacting is transient on both paths): all pass --

waits through compaction and re-steers the active requester (86566)
waits through compaction on the completion handoff wake (86566)
does not retry non-compacting steer failures (86566)
Tests  6 passed | 126 skipped (132)


Observed result after fix: before the fix a compacting outcome on either the steer path or the generated-completion wake routes the completion to the direct fallback path (`path: "direct"`); after the fix each path waits through compaction and re-attempts the same wake, so the completion stays on the steering path (`path: "steered"`) and the queue is attempted twice. A non-compacting failure (`no_active_run`) is attempted only once and is not retried. The full subagent-announce-delivery suite (132 tests) passes.

What was not tested: no live multi-agent run with a real compaction cycle was exercised; the compacting-then-ready transition is simulated through the queue-outcome dependency on the real steering and completion code paths. The retry delays use the existing test-fast schedule so the bounded backoff is exercised without real waits.

target.title: fix(qa): reduce WhatsApp live RTT setup cost
target.body:
Summary
- Register WhatsApp through a narrow runtime setter entrypoint so gateway startup no longer imports the broad WhatsApp runtime barrel before live message handling.
- Add WhatsApp live QA retry handling for logged-out Convex credentials; stale leases stay held while the lane tries alternate pooled credentials.
- Add a maintainer-role workflow input for WhatsApp Convex leases, and switch QA gateway-child readiness from `config.get` to lightweight `health` RPC to avoid loading the full config schema during startup checks.
- Keep remote changed gates on the Corepack pnpm shim in Blacksmith runner shells so Testbox `pnpm check:changed` is stable even when launched directly through Crabbox.
- Keep the existing WhatsApp RTT instrumentation, heap snapshots, gateway traces, and changelog coverage on this branch.

Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/whatsapp/index.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-runtime-api-guardrails.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/whatsapp/whatsapp-live.runtime.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qa-lab/src/gateway-child.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/whatsapp/whatsapp-live.runtime.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/gateway-child.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/index.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-runtime-api-guardrails.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/changed-lanes.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs -c .oxlintrc.json --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/whatsapp/index.ts extensions/whatsapp/index.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/runtime-setter-api.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-runtime-api-guardrails.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs -c .oxlintrc.json --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/whatsapp/whatsapp-live.runtime.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/whatsapp/whatsapp-live.runtime.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs -c .oxlintrc.json --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/qa-lab/src/gateway-child.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/gateway-child.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs -c .oxlintrc.json scripts/check-changed.mjs test/scripts/changed-lanes.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- Testbox-through-Crabbox changed gate: `tbx_01ks94abkaf4rjwh7hemswwb6t`, Actions run `26318665070`, `pnpm check:changed` at `8f1403f35b3` passed.
- Testbox-through-Crabbox changed gate: `tbx_01ks9amfdrepmf5w8bt3yt4ajd`, Actions run `26321093857`, `corepack pnpm check:changed` at `1c6acf13de8` passed.

Live evidence
- `26315828755` at `c2bad2a4099`: WhatsApp job failed before RTT; CI Convex credential fingerprint `6b2d34243bac` was logged out (`401 Unauthorized`).
- `26316577807` at `9897bcfe79a`: maintainer role failed before RTT because `OPENCLAW_QA_CONVEX_SECRET_MAINTAINER` is not configured.
- `26317166666` at `57a09eee274`: retry path held the logged-out CI credential and attempted another lease; broker exhausted after 360000ms, proving the CI WhatsApp pool currently has no alternate healthy credential.
- `26321264487` at `1c6acf13de8`: latest WhatsApp artifact `qa-live-whatsapp-26321264487-1` shows the same CI credential fingerprint `6b2d34243bac`, logged out, then Convex credential pool exhaustion after 360000ms with no observed messages.
- Repo secret listing confirms `OPENCLAW_QA_CONVEX_SECRET_CI` and `OPENCLAW_QA_CONVEX_SITE_URL` exist, but `OPENCLAW_QA_CONVEX_SECRET_MAINTAINER` is absent.

Behavior addressed: WhatsApp live QA now avoids one eager runtime import path, reports stale Convex WhatsApp credentials without silently reacquiring the same row, skips full config-schema work for gateway-child RPC readiness, preserves retry auth directories across successful alternate credential startup, and keeps direct Crabbox/Testbox changed gates off stale runner pnpm shims.
Real environment tested: GitHub Actions live WhatsApp Convex QA on Blacksmith runners, Testbox-through-Crabbox changed gate, plus targeted local Vitest/oxlint/diff checks in the branch worktree.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `qa-live-transports-convex.yml` with `ref=whatsapp-rtt-leak`, `whatsapp_scenario=whatsapp-canary`, `whatsapp_credential_role=ci`, `matrix_profile=fast`; Testbox-through-Crabbox `corepack pnpm check:changed` on `whatsapp-rtt-leak` after deepening `origin/main`.
Evidence after fix: stale credential fingerprint `6b2d34243bac` is identified and held; alternate acquisition exhausts, so current live RTT proof is blocked by credential pool state rather than an unclassified harness failure; `pnpm check:changed` is green at `1c6acf13de8`.
Observed result after fix: branch improves startup/QA hygiene and credential diagnosis, but it does not yet prove the WhatsApp RTT target; the only CI WhatsApp credential is logged out.
What was not tested: a successful live WhatsApp RTT run after `1c6acf13de8`; current Convex credential state blocks that proof.
